Description:This Security Technical Implementation Guide is published as a tool to improve the security of Department of Defense (DOD) information systems. The requirements are derived from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) 800-53 and related documents. Comments or proposed revisions to this document should be sent via email to the following address: disa.stig_spt@mail.mil.

    • V-253382 - Solicited Remote Assistance must not be allowed. - Fail
    • V-253386 - Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices. - Fail
    • V-253387 - The default autorun behavior must be configured to prevent autorun commands. - Fail
    • V-253388 - Autoplay must be disabled for all drives. - Fail

    • V-253416 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Basic authentication. - Fail
    • V-253418 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not use Basic authentication. - Fail
    • V-253454 - Anonymous enumeration of shares must be restricted. - Fail

      • V-253349 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Failures. - Fail
      • V-253350 - Camera access from the lock screen must be disabled. - Fail
      • V-253357 - Local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain systems. - Fail
      • V-253359 - Run as different user must be removed from context menus. - Fail
      • V-253360 - Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled. - Fail
      • V-253362 - Hardened UNC Paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares. - Fail
      • V-253363 - Windows 11 must be configured to prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first. - Fail
      • V-253365 - Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked. - Fail
      • V-253368 - Windows 11 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials. - Fail
      • V-253369 - Virtualization-based Security must be enabled on Windows 11 with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection. - Fail
      • V-253372 - Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers. - Fail
      • V-253373 - Group Policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed. - Fail
      • V-253374 - Downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be prevented. - Fail
      • V-253375 - Web publishing and online ordering wizards must be prevented from downloading a list of providers. - Fail
      • V-253376 - Printing over HTTP must be prevented. - Fail
      • V-253378 - The network selection user interface (UI) must not be displayed on the logon screen. - Fail
      • V-253379 - Local users on domain-joined computers must not be enumerated. - Fail
      • V-253383 - Unauthenticated RPC clients must be restricted from connecting to the RPC server. - Fail
      • V-253389 - Enhanced anti-spoofing for facial recognition must be enabled on Windows 11. - Fail
      • V-253391 - Administrator accounts must not be enumerated during elevation. - Fail
      • V-253395 - The Microsoft Defender SmartScreen for Explorer must be enabled. - Fail
      • V-253401 - Windows 11 must be configured to require a minimum pin length of six characters or greater. - Fail
      • V-253404 - Remote Desktop Services must always prompt a client for passwords upon connection. - Fail
      • V-253407 - Attachments must be prevented from being downloaded from RSS feeds. - Fail
      • V-253409 - Indexing of encrypted files must be turned off. - Fail
      • V-253410 - Users must be prevented from changing installation options. - Fail
      • V-253415 - PowerShell Transcription must be enabled on Windows 11. - Fail
      • V-253417 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not allow unencrypted traffic. - Fail
      • V-253419 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not allow unencrypted traffic. - Fail
      • V-253420 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not store RunAs credentials. - Fail
      • V-253421 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Digest authentication. - Fail
      • V-253422 - Windows 11 must be configured to prevent Windows apps from being activated by voice while the system is locked. - Fail
      • V-253424 - Windows Ink Workspace must be configured to disallow access above the lock. - Fail
      • V-253427 - The DoD Root CA certificates must be installed in the Trusted Root Store. - Fail
      • V-253428 - The External Root CA certificates must be installed in the Trusted Root Store on unclassified systems. - Fail
      • V-253429 - The DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems. - Fail
      • V-253430 - The US DOD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems. - Fail
      • V-253435 - The built-in administrator account must be renamed. - Fail
      • V-253436 - The built-in guest account must be renamed. - Fail
      • V-253444 - The machine inactivity limit must be set to 15 minutes, locking the system with the screensaver. - Fail
      • V-253445 - The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon. - Fail
      • V-253448 - The Smart Card removal option must be configured to Force Logoff or Lock Workstation. - Fail
      • V-253457 - Remote calls to the Security Account Manager (SAM) must be restricted to Administrators. - Fail
      • V-253458 - NTLM must be prevented from falling back to a Null session. - Fail
      • V-253459 - PKU2U authentication using online identities must be prevented. - Fail
      • V-253464 - The system must be configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients. - Fail
      • V-253465 - The system must be configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based servers. - Fail
      • V-253466 - The system must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing. - Fail
      • V-253469 - User Account Control must prompt administrators for consent on the secure desktop. - Fail
      • V-253470 - Windows 11 must use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged and nonprivileged accounts. - Fail
      • V-253471 - User Account Control must automatically deny elevation requests for standard users. - Fail
      • V-253476 - Passwords for enabled local Administrator accounts must be changed at least every 60 days. - Fail
      • V-253480 - The "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups. - Fail
      • V-253482 - The "Allow log on locally" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Users groups. - Fail
      • V-253483 - The "Back up files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group. - Fail
      • V-253491 - The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems. - Fail
      • V-253492 - The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts. - Fail
      • V-253493 - The "Deny log on as a service" user right on Windows 11 domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts. - Fail
      • V-253494 - The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems. - Fail
      • V-253495 - The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows 11 workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems. - Fail
      • V-253505 - The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group. - Fail

        Manual Checks

        Detailed Results: High Severity (CAT I)

          V-253260 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253260r958872_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000031
          Identities:CCI-002476 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-28 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-28 (1))
          Description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          For virtual desktop implementations (VDIs) in which the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

          For AVD implementations with no data at rest, this is NA.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE\

          Value Name: UseAdvancedStartup
          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000001 (1)

          If one of the following registry values does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE\

          Value Name: UseTPMPIN
          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000001 (1)

          Value Name: UseTPMKeyPIN
          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000001 (1)

          When BitLocker network unlock is used:

          Value Name: UseTPMPIN
          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000002 (2)

          Value Name: UseTPMKeyPIN
          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000002 (2)

          BitLocker network unlock may be used in conjunction with a BitLocker PIN. See the article below regarding information about network unlock.

          https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/information-protection/bitlocker/bitlocker-how-to-enable-network-unlock
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> BitLocker Drive Encryption >> Operating System Drives "Require additional authentication at startup" to "Enabled" with "Configure TPM Startup PIN:" set to "Require startup PIN with TPM" or with "Configure TPM startup key and PIN:" set to "Require startup key and PIN with TPM".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253260
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000031 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication.
          Description:If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                    • false ('Configure TPM startup PIN' is set to 'Require startup PIN with TPM'.)
                    • false ('Configure TPM startup key and PIN' is set to 'Require startup key and PIN with TPM'.)
                  • true ('Allow network unlock at startup' is Enabled.) (negated)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                    • true ('Configure TPM startup PIN' is set to 'Allow startup PIN with TPM'.)
                    • true ('Configure TPM startup key and PIN' is set to 'Allow startup key and PIN with TPM'.)
                  • false ('Allow network unlock at startup' is Enabled.)
              • true ('Require additional authentication at startup' is Enabled.)
          Tests:

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25326001 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'UseAdvancedStartup'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'

          V-253284 - Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253284r958928_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000150
          Identities:CCI-002824 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-16; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-16)
          Description: Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) blocks exploits that use the Structured Exception Handling overwrite technique, a common buffer overflow attack.
          Check Text:Verify SEHOP is turned on.

          If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\kernel\

          Value Name: DisableExceptionChainValidation

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000000 (0)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Enable Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP)" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253284
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000150 - Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) must be enabled.
          Description:Attackers are constantly looking for vulnerabilities in systems and applications. Structured Exception Handling Overwrite Protection (SEHOP) blocks exploits that use the Structured Exception Handling overwrite technique, a common buffer overflow attack.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (SEHOP is turned on and properly configured)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:SEHOP is turned on and properly configured
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\kernel'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'DisableExceptionChainValidation'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\kernel\DisableExceptionChainValidation'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253382 - Solicited Remote Assistance must not be allowed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253382r1137695_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000155
          Identities:CCI-001090 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-4.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-4)
          Description: Remote assistance allows another user to view or take control of the local session of a user. Solicited assistance is help that is specifically requested by the local user. This may allow unauthorized parties access to the resources on the computer.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\

          Value Name: fAllowToGetHelp

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Remote Assistance >> "Configure Solicited Remote Assistance" to "Disabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253382
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000155 - Solicited Remote Assistance must not be allowed.
          Description:Remote assistance allows another user to view or take control of the local session of a user. Solicited assistance is help that is specifically requested by the local user. This may allow unauthorized parties access to the resources on the computer.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Configure Solicited Remote Assistance' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Configure Solicited Remote Assistance' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'fAllowToGetHelp'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\fAllowToGetHelp'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253386 - Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253386r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000180
          Identities:CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. This setting will disable autoplay for non-volume devices (such as Media Transfer Protocol (MTP) devices).
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\

          Value Name: NoAutoplayfornonVolume

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253386
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000180 - Autoplay must be turned off for non-volume devices.
          Description:Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. This setting will disable autoplay for non-volume devices (such as Media Transfer Protocol (MTP) devices).
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Disallow Autoplay for non-volume devices' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoAutoplayfornonVolume'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer\NoAutoplayfornonVolume'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253387 - The default autorun behavior must be configured to prevent autorun commands.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253387r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000185
          Identities:CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing autorun commands to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Configuring this setting prevents autorun commands from executing.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\

          Value Name: NoAutorun

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Set the default behavior for AutoRun" to "Enabled:Do not execute any autorun commands".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253387
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000185 - The default autorun behavior must be configured to prevent autorun commands.
          Description:Allowing autorun commands to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Configuring this setting prevents autorun commands from executing.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Default behavior for AutoRun' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Do not execute any autorun commands' selected)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Default behavior for AutoRun' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Do not execute any autorun commands' selected
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoAutorun'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\NoAutorun'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253388 - Autoplay must be disabled for all drives.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253388r958804_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000190
          Identities:CCI-001764 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (2))
          Description: Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. By default, autoplay is disabled on removable drives, such as the floppy disk drive (but not the CD-ROM drive) and on network drives. If this policy is enabled, autoplay can be disabled on all drives.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\policies\Explorer\

          Value Name: NoDriveTypeAutoRun

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x000000ff (255)

          Note: If the value for NoDriveTypeAutorun is entered manually, it must be entered as "ff" when Hexadecimal is selected, or "255" with Decimal selected. Using the policy value specified in the Fix section will enter it correctly.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> AutoPlay Policies >> "Turn off AutoPlay" to "Enabled:All Drives".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253388
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000190 - Autoplay must be disabled for all drives.
          Description:Allowing autoplay to execute may introduce malicious code to a system. Autoplay begins reading from a drive as soon as media is inserted in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs or music on audio media may start. By default, autoplay is disabled on removable drives, such as the floppy disk drive (but not the CD-ROM drive) and on network drives. If this policy is enabled, autoplay can be disabled on all drives.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Turn off AutoPlay' is set to 'Enabled:All Drives')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Turn off AutoPlay' is set to 'Enabled:All Drives'
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoDriveTypeAutoRun'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25338800 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 255
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '255'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\NoDriveTypeAutoRun'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253411 - The Windows Installer feature "Always install with elevated privileges" must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253411r1051054_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000315
          Identities:CCI-003980 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-11 (2))
          CCI-001812 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-11 (2))
          Description: Standard user accounts must not be granted elevated privileges. Enabling Windows Installer to elevate privileges when installing applications can allow malicious persons and applications to gain full control of a system.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer\

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0

          Result:false
          Title:'Always install with elevated privileges' is set to 'Disabled'

          V-253416 - The Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Basic authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253416r958510_rule

          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000330

          Version:WN11-CC-000345

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Service\

          Value Name: AllowBasic

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Service >> "Allow Basic authentication" to "Disabled".

          Severity Override Guidance: The AO can allow the severity override if they have reviewed the overall protection. This would only be allowed temporarily for implementation as documented and approved.
          ....
          Allowing Basic authentication to be used for the sole creation of Office 365 DoD tenants.
          ....
          A documented mechanism and or script that can disable Basic authentication once administration completes.
          ....
          Use of a Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) and adherence to the Clean Source principle for administration.
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:

            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))

          Tests:

          V-253454 - Anonymous enumeration of shares must be restricted.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253454r1137695_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000150
          Identities:CCI-001090 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-4.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-4)
          Description: Allowing anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all account names and enumerate all shared resources can provide a map of potential points to attack the system.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\

          Value Name: RestrictAnonymous

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares" to "Enabled".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253454
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000150 - Anonymous enumeration of shares must be restricted.
          Description:Allowing anonymous logon users (null session connections) to list all account names and enumerate all shared resources can provide a map of potential points to attack the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Network access: Do not allow anonymous enumeration of SAM accounts and shares' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'restrictanonymous'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'restrictanonymous'
          • last_write_time equals '134188485430000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\restrictanonymous\value = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

          V-253462 - The LanMan authentication level must be set to send NTLMv2 response only, and to refuse LM and NTLM.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253462r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000205
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The Kerberos v5 authentication protocol is the default for authentication of users who are logging on to domain accounts. NTLM, which is less secure, is retained in later Windows versions for compatibility with clients and servers that are running earlier versions of Windows or applications that still use it. It is also used to authenticate logons to stand-alone computers that are running later versions.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\

          Value Name: LmCompatibilityLevel

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 5
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: LAN Manager authentication level" to "Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM".
          Severity:high
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253462
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000205 - The LanMan authentication level must be set to send NTLMv2 response only, and to refuse LM and NTLM.
          Description:The Kerberos v5 authentication protocol is the default for authentication of users who are logging on to domain accounts. NTLM, which is less secure, is retained in later Windows versions for compatibility with clients and servers that are running earlier versions of Windows or applications that still use it. It is also used to authenticate logons to stand-alone computers that are running later versions.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Network security: LAN Manager authentication level' is set to 'Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM' (Level 5))
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Network security: LAN Manager authentication level' is set to 'Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM' (Level 5)
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LmCompatibilityLevel'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25346200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 5
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '5'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • name equals 'LmCompatibilityLevel'
          • last_write_time equals '134188485430000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '4'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\LmCompatibilityLevel\value = '4' failed test of 'equals' compared to '5'

        Detailed Results: Medium Severity (CAT II)

          V-253261 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN with a minimum length of six digits for pre-boot authentication.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253261r958504_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000032
          Identities:CCI-000804 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-8.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-8)
          Description: If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives. Increasing the pin length requires a greater number of guesses for an attacker.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          For virtual desktop implementations (VDIs) in which the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

          For AVD implementations with no data at rest, this is NA.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE\

          Value Name: MinimumPIN
          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000006 (6) or greater
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> BitLocker Drive Encryption >> Operating System Drives "Configure minimum PIN length for startup" to "Enabled" with "Minimum characters:" set to "6" or greater.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253261
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000032 - Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN with a minimum length of six digits for pre-boot authentication.
          Description:If data at rest is unencrypted, it is vulnerable to disclosure. Even if the operating system enforces permissions on data access, an adversary can remove non-volatile memory and read it directly, thereby circumventing operating system controls. Encrypting the data ensures that confidentiality is protected even when the operating system is not running. Pre-boot authentication prevents unauthorized users from accessing encrypted drives. Increasing the pin length requires a greater number of guesses for an attacker.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (BitLocker must be 6 digits or more.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25326100 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:BitLocker must be 6 digits or more.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25326100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check for BitLocker MinimumPIN
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MinimumPIN'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25326100 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '6'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE\MinimumPIN'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253273 - Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253273r1051040_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000090
          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          Description: Passwords that do not expire increase exposure with a greater probability of being discovered or cracked.
          Check Text:Run "Computer Management".
          Navigate to System Tools >> Local Users and Groups >> Users.
          Double-click each active account.

          If "Password never expires" is selected for any account, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure all passwords to expire.
          Run "Computer Management".
          Navigate to System Tools >> Local Users and Groups >> Users.
          Double-click each active account.
          Ensure "Password never expires" is not checked on all active accounts.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253273
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.
          Description:Passwords that do not expire increase exposure with a greater probability of being discovered or cracked.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25327301 (cmdlet_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Accounts must be configured to require password expiration.
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25327301 (cmdlet_object)
          Object Comment:Enabled Local Accounts with PasswordExpires=False should not exist
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'module_name' must be equal to 'CimCmdlets'
          • check = 'verb' must be equal to 'Get'
          • check = 'noun' must be equal to 'CimInstance'
          • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
            • class must be equal to 'Win32_Useraccount'
            • filter must be equal to 'PasswordExpires=False and LocalAccount=True and Disabled=false'
          • for 'select' the following must be true:
            • unique must be equal to 'Name,PasswordExpires,Disabled,LocalAccount'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ exists ]
          • module_name equals 'CimCmdlets'
          • module_id equals '{fb6cc51d-c096-4b38-b78d-0fed6277096a}'
          • module_version equals '1.0.0.0'
          • verb equals 'Get'
          • noun equals 'CimInstance'
          • collected 'parameters' result:
            • class = 'Win32_Useraccount'
            • filter = 'PasswordExpires=False and LocalAccount=True and Disabled=false'
          • collected 'select' result:
            • unique = 'Name,PasswordExpires,Disabled,LocalAccount'
          • collected 'value' result:
            • name = 'Administrator'
            • passwordexpires = 'False'
            • disabled = 'False'
            • localaccount = 'True'
          • collected 'value' result:
            • name = 'DCProtectService'
            • passwordexpires = 'False'
            • disabled = 'False'
            • localaccount = 'True'
          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'none_exists' and 1 collected item exists, so result is false

          V-253280 - Software certificate installation files must be removed from Windows 11.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253280r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000130
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Use of software certificates and their accompanying installation files for end users to access resources is less secure than the use of hardware-based certificates.
          Check Text:Search all drives for *.p12 and *.pfx files.

          If any files with these extensions exist, this is a finding.

          This does not apply to server-based applications that have a requirement for .p12 certificate files (e.g., Oracle Wallet Manager) or Adobe PreFlight certificate files. Some applications create files with extensions of .p12 that are not certificate installation files. Removal of non-certificate installation files from systems is not required. These must be documented with the ISSO.
          Fix Text:Remove any certificate installation files (*.p12 and *.pfx) found on a system.

          Note: This does not apply to server-based applications that have a requirement for .p12 certificate files (e.g., Oracle Wallet Manager) or Adobe PreFlight certificate files.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253280
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Software certificate installation files must be removed from Windows 11.
          Description:Use of software certificates and their accompanying installation files for end users to access resources is less secure than the use of hardware-based certificates.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Software certificate installation files must be removed. Remove .p12 files)
            • true (Software certificate installation files must be removed. Remove .pfx files)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25328001 (file_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Software certificate installation files must be removed. Remove .p12 files
          Check Existence:No collected items may exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25328001 (file_object)
          Object Comment:Search for .p12 files
          Object Requirements:
          • behavior requirements:
            • recurse_direction = down
          • check = 'path' must match the pattern '.*'
          • check = 'filename' must match the pattern '.[Pp]12$'

          V-253289 - The Secondary Logon service must be disabled on Windows 11.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253289r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000175
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: The Secondary Logon service provides a means for entering alternate credentials, typically used to run commands with elevated privileges. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft.
          Check Text:Run "Services.msc".

          Locate the "Secondary Logon" service.

          If the "Startup Type" is not "Disabled" or the "Status" is "Running", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the "Secondary Logon" service "Startup Type" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253289
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-00-000175 - The Secondary Logon service must be disabled on Windows 11.
          Description:The Secondary Logon service provides a means for entering alternate credentials, typically used to run commands with elevated privileges. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The Secondary Logon service Startup Type is Disabled or the service is uninstalled)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25328900 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The Secondary Logon service Startup Type is Disabled or the service is uninstalled
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25328900 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:Secondary Logon Service Startup Type
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='seclogon''
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25328900 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Service Startup Type is Disabled
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all startmode must be equal to 'Disabled'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
          • wql equals 'SELECT startmode FROM Win32_Service WHERE name='seclogon''
          • collected 'result' result:
            • startmode = 'Manual'
          Result Analysis:startmode = 'Manual' failed test of 'equals' compared to 'Disabled'

          V-253291 - Bluetooth must be turned off unless approved by the organization.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253291r1153422_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:bluetoothinstallation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000210
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: If not configured properly, Bluetooth may allow rogue devices to communicate with a system. If a rogue device is paired with a system, there is potential for sensitive information to be compromised.
          Check Text:This is NA if the system does not have Bluetooth.

          Verify the Bluetooth radio is turned off unless approved by the organization. If it is not, this is a finding.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\device\Connectivity\

          Value Name: AllowBluetooth

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000000 (0)

          Approval must be documented with the ISSO.
          Fix Text:Turn off Bluetooth radios not organizationally approved.

          For systems managed by Intune, apply the DOD Windows 11 STIG Settings Catalog (or equivalent Intune policy) found in the Intune policy package available on cyber.mil.
          Steps to create an Intune policy:
          1. Sign in to the Intune admin center >> Devices >> Configuration >> Create >> New Policy.
          2. Platform: Windows 10 and later. Profile type: Settings Catalog, then click "Create".
          3. Basics: Provide a Name and Description of the profile, then click "Next".
          4. Configuration settings: Click "+ Add settings" and search for connectivity under the Settings picker. Under the Connectivity category, check the box next to Allow Bluetooth setting. Choose the first option, "Disallow Bluetooth", then click "Next".
          5. Scope tags: (optional), then click "Next".
          6. Assignments: Assign the policy to Entra security groups that contain the target users or devices, then click "Next".
          7. Review + create: Review the deployment summary, then click "Create".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253291
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Bluetooth must be turned off unless approved by the organization.
          Description:If not configured properly, Bluetooth may allow rogue devices to communicate with a system. If a rogue device is paired with a system, there is potential for sensitive information to be compromised.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Bluetooth radio must be turned off.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25329101 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Bluetooth radio must be turned off.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25329101 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Verify the Bluetooth radio is turned off
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\device\Connectivity'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowBluetooth'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25329101 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 0
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\current\device\Connectivity\AllowBluetooth'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253293 - The system must notify the user when a Bluetooth device attempts to connect.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253293r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:bluetoothinstallation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-00-000230
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: If not configured properly, Bluetooth may allow rogue devices to communicate with a system. If a rogue device is paired with a system, there is potential for sensitive information to be compromised.
          Check Text:This is NA if the system does not have Bluetooth, or if Bluetooth is turned off per the organizations policy.

          Search for "Bluetooth".
          View Bluetooth Settings.
          Select "More Bluetooth Options"
          If "Alert me when a new Bluetooth device wants to connect" is not checked, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure Bluetooth to notify users if devices attempt to connect.
          View Bluetooth Settings.
          Ensure "Alert me when a new Bluetooth device wants to connect" is checked.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253293
          Definition Result:false
          Title:The system must notify the user when a Bluetooth device attempts to connect.
          Description:The system must notify the user when a Bluetooth device attempts to connect.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (The system must notify the user when a Bluetooth device attempts to connect.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25329301 (ntuser_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The system must notify the user when a Bluetooth device attempts to connect.
          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25329301 (ntuser_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieve AcceptIncomingRequests from BluetoothAuthenticationAgent
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\BluetoothAuthenticationAgent'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AcceptIncomingRequests'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25329301 (ntuser_state)
          State Comment:Value equal to 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-1565594376-788080289-153769433-156119'
          • username equals 'MAXHEALTH\katessem.admin'
          • account_type equals 'domain'
          • logged_on equals 'true'
          • days_since_last_logon equals '0'
          • enabled equals 'true'
          • date_modified equals '134193435850000000'
          • days_since_modified equals '3'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\katessem.admin\ntuser.dat'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-1565594376-788080289-153769433-157727'
          • username equals 'MAXHEALTH\katessem'
          • account_type equals 'domain'
          • logged_on equals 'true'
          • days_since_last_logon equals '0'
          • enabled equals 'true'
          • date_modified equals '134189371180000000'
          • days_since_modified equals '7'
          • filepath equals 'C:\Users\katessem\ntuser.dat'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ does not exist ]
          • key does not exist
          • sid equals 'S-1-5-21-2056937484-830314662-3505637335-500'
          • username equals 'MHL-PW06M2V2\Administrator'

          • logged_on equals 'false'

          For 'MAXHEALTH\katessem.admin': Registry Name Not found in user profile ntuser.dat file(s): 'Software\Microsoft\BluetoothAuthenticationAgent\AcceptIncomingRequests'.

          V-253298 - The number of allowed bad logon attempts must be configured to three or less.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253298r958388_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000010
          Identities:CCI-000044 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 a)
          Description: The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. The higher this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system. The number of bad logon attempts must be reasonably small to minimize the possibility of a successful password attack, while allowing for honest errors made during a normal user logon.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy.

          If the "Account lockout threshold" is "0" or more than "3" attempts, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Account Lockout Policy >> "Account lockout threshold" to "3" or less invalid logon attempts (excluding "0" which is unacceptable).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253298
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AC-000010 - The number of allowed bad logon attempts must be configured to three or less.
          Description:The account lockout feature, when enabled, prevents brute-force password attacks on the system. The higher this value is, the less effective the account lockout feature will be in protecting the local system. The number of bad logon attempts must be reasonably small to minimize the possibility of a successful password attack, while allowing for honest errors made during a normal user logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Account lockout threshold' is set to '3' or less)
              • true ('Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329800 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is set to '3' or less
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329800 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is less than or equal to 3
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_threshold' must be less than or equal to '3'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • force_logoff equals '1'
          • lockout_duration equals '1800'
          • lockout_observation_window equals '900'
          • lockout_threshold equals '5'
          Result Analysis:lockout_threshold = '5' failed test of 'less than or equal' compared to '3'
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_test)
          Result:true
          Title:'Account lockout threshold' is not set to '0'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000001 (lockoutpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25329801 (lockoutpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Lockout Threshold is greater than 0
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'lockout_threshold' must be greater than '0'

          V-253303 - Passwords must, at a minimum, be 14 characters.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253303r1051044_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AC-000035
          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000205 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (i); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
          Description: Information systems not protected with strong password schemes (including passwords of minimum length) provide the opportunity for anyone to crack the password, thus gaining access to the system and compromising the device, information, or the local network.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy.

          If the value for the "Minimum password length," is less than "14" characters, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Password Policy >> "Minimum password length" to "14" characters.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253303
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AC-000035 - Passwords must, at a minimum, be 14 characters.
          Description:Information systems not protected with strong password schemes (including passwords of minimum length) provide the opportunity for anyone to crack the password, thus gaining access to the system and compromising the device, information, or the local network.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Minimum password length' is set to at least '14' characters)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330300 (passwordpolicy_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Minimum password length' is set to at least '14' characters
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000002 (passwordpolicy_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330300 (passwordpolicy_state)
          State Comment:Minimum Password Length is greater than or equal to 14
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'min_passwd_len' must be greater than or equal to '14'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • max_passwd_age equals '5184000'
          • min_passwd_age equals '86400'
          • min_passwd_len equals '8'
          • password_hist_len equals '24'
          • password_complexity equals '1'
          • reversible_encryption equals '0'
          • anonymous_name_lookup equals '0'
          Result Analysis:min_passwd_len = '8' failed test of 'greater than or equal' compared to '14'

          V-253308 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253308r971541_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000030
          Identities:CCI-001914 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 (3); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 (3))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security Group Management records events such as creating, deleting or changing of security groups, including changes in group members.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Account Management >> Security Group Management - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit Security Group Management" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253308
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000030 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - Security Group Management successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security Group Management records events such as creating, deleting or changing of security groups, including changes in group members.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Audit Security Group Management' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Security Group Management' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'security_group_management' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330801 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'security_group_management' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
