          • audit_detailedtracking_tokenrightadjusted equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          Result Analysis:security_group_management = 'AUDIT_NONE' failed test of 'equals' compared to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          security_group_management = 'AUDIT_NONE' failed test of 'equals' compared to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          V-253309 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253309r958566_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000035
          Identities:CCI-001314 (NIST SP 800-53: SI-11 c; NIST SP 800-53A: SI-11.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-11 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-11 b)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Account Management >> User Account Management - Failure
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit User Account Management" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253309
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000035 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Failure')
              • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25330900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25330900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'User Account Management' is set to 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'user_account_management' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

          V-253310 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253310r991551_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000040
          Identities:CCI-001403 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Account Management >> User Account Management - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> "Audit User Account Management" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253310
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000040 - The system must be configured to audit Account Management - User Account Management successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          User Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling user accounts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success')
              • false ('Audit User Account Management' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')

          V-253311 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253311r1051047_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000045
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-003938 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-5 (1) (b))
          CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Plug and Play activity records events related to the successful connection of external devices.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Detailed Tracking >> Plug and Play Events - Success
          Fix Text:Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit PNP Activity" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253311
          Definition Result:false
          Title:The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - PNP Activity successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Plug and Play activity records events related to the successful connection of external devices.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled)
            • false (Detailed Tracking - Plug and Play Events successes.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25331101 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25331101 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Secure Boot State Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25331101 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25331102 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Detailed Tracking - Plug and Play Events successes.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25331102 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieves a auditeventpolicysubcategories_object.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25331102 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:pnp_activity (Plug and Play Events) subcategory must be Success.
          State Requirements:
          • for allpnp_activity, all of the following must be true:
            • pnp_activity must match the pattern 'AUDIT_(SUCCESS|SUCCESS_FAILURE)'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • directory_service_changes equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • account_lockout equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_extended_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_quick_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logoff equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253312 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253312r1051048_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000050
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          CCI-003938 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-5 (1) (b))
          CCI-001814 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-5 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Process creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Detailed Tracking >> Process Creation - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> "Audit Process Creation" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253312
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000050 - The system must be configured to audit Detailed Tracking - Process Creation successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Process creation records events related to the creation of a process and the source.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Audit - Process Creation - Success only)
              • false (Audit - Process Creation - Success and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit - Process Creation - Success only
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'process_creation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          V-253313 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253313r991578_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000054
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Account Lockout events can be used to identify potentially malicious logon attempts.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:

          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").

          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Logon/Logoff >> Account Lockout - Failure
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Account Lockout" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253313
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000054 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Account Lockout failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Account Lockout events can be used to identify potentially malicious logon attempts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Account Lockout' is set to 'Failure')
              • false ('Audit Account Lockout' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:tst:25331300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Account Lockout' is set to 'Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          • Collect any available items.

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'account_lockout' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

          V-253314 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253314r991570_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000060
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Group Membership records information related to the group membership of a user's logon token.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Logon/Logoff >> Group Membership - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Group Membership" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253314
          Definition Result:false
          Title:The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Group Membership records information related to the group membership of a user's logon token.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled)
            • false (Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25331401 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25331401 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Secure Boot State Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25331401 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25331402 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Logon/Logoff - Group Membership successes.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25331402 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieves a auditeventpolicysubcategories_object.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25331402 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:group_membership subcategory must be Success.
          State Requirements:
          • for allgroup_membership, all of the following must be true:
            • group_membership must match the pattern 'AUDIT_(SUCCESS|SUCCESS_FAILURE)'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253315 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logoff successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253315r958406_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000065
          Identities:CCI-000067 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (1).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logoff records user logoffs. If this is an interactive logoff, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Logon/Logoff >> Logoff - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Logoff" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253315
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000065 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Logoff successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Logoff records user logoffs. If this is an interactive logoff, it is recorded on the local system. If it is to a network share, it is recorded on the system accessed.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Audit - Logoff - Success only)
              • false (Audit - Logoff - Success and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit - Logoff - Success only
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          State Comment:Audit - Logoff - Success only
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'logoff' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          V-253318 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Special Logon successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253318r991578_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000080
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Special Logon records special logons which have administrative privileges and can be used to elevate processes.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Logon/Logoff >> Special Logon - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Special Logon" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253318
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000080 - The system must be configured to audit Logon/Logoff - Special Logon successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Special Logon records special logons which have administrative privileges and can be used to elevate processes.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Audit - Special Logon - Success only)
              • false (Audit - Special Logon - Success and Failure)

          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25331800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)

          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'special_logon' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          • security_state_change equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • security_system_extension equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • system_integrity equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • group_membership equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • pnp_activity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • user_device_claims equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • audit_detailedtracking_tokenrightadjusted equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          Result Analysis:special_logon = 'AUDIT_NONE' failed test of 'equals' compared to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-253320 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253320r991572_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000082
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:

          Open PowerShell or a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following:

          Object Access >> File Share - Success

          If the system does not audit the above, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File Share" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253320
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000082 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - File Share successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing file shares records events related to connection to shares on a system including system shares such as C$.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Audit File Share' is set to 'Success')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit File Share' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'File Share' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'file_share' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25331901 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'File Share' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'file_share' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-253321 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Object Access - Other Object Access Events successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253321r991572_rule

          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing for other object access records events related to the management of task scheduler jobs and COM+ objects.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.
          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:

          Open PowerShell or a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").

          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following:

          Object Access >> Other Object Access Events - Success

          If the system does not audit the above, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Other Object Access Events" with "Success" selected.

          Weight:10.0

          V-253323 - The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253323r991583_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000085
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Object Access >> Removable Storage - Failure

          Some virtual machines may generate excessive audit events for access to the virtual hard disk itself when this setting is enabled. This may be set to Not Configured in such cases and would not be a finding. This must be documented with the ISSO to include mitigations such as monitoring or restricting any actual removable storage connected to the VM.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Removable Storage" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253323
          Definition Result:false
          Title:The system must be configured to audit Object Access - Removable Storage failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Auditing object access for removable media records events related to access attempts on file system objects on removable storage devices.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • true (Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled)
            • false (Removable Storage - Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25332301 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings must be set to Enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25332301 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Secure Boot State Setting
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25332301 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25332302 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Removable Storage - Failure
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25332302 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment:Retrieves a auditeventpolicysubcategories_object.
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25332302 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:removable storage subcategory must be Failure.
          State Requirements:
          • for allremovable_storage, all of the following must be true:
            • removable_storage must match the pattern 'AUDIT_(FAILURE|SUCCESS_FAILURE)'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253326 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authentication Policy Change successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253326r991572_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000105

          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authentication Policy Change records events related to changes in authentication policy including Kerberos policy and Trust changes.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.
          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Policy Change >> Authentication Policy Change - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Authentication Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:

          V-253327 - The system must be configured to audit Policy Change - Authorization Policy Change successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253327r991572_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000107
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as create a token object.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          -Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          -Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding.

          Policy Change >> Authorization Policy Change - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> "Audit Authorization Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253327
          Definition Result:false

Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Authorization Policy Change records events related to changes in user rights, such as create a token object.

              • false (Audit - Audit Policy Change - Success and Failure)

          V-253328 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253328r958732_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000110
          Identities:CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Privilege Use >> Sensitive Privilege Use - Failure
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253328
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000110 - The system must be configured to audit Privilege Use - Sensitive Privilege Use failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Sensitive Privilege Use records events related to use of sensitive privileges, such as "Act as part of the operating system" or "Debug programs".
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25332800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sensitive_privilege_use' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25332801 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sensitive_privilege_use' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-253331 - The system must be configured to audit System - Other System Events successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253331r991579_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000130
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other System Events records information related to cryptographic key operations and the Windows Firewall service.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*"

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          System >> Other System Events - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Other System Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253331
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000130 - The system must be configured to audit System - Other System Events successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other System Events records information related to cryptographic key operations and the Windows Firewall service.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Other System Events' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:

          Result:false
          Title:'Other System Events' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333100 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_system_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333101 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)

          V-253333 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security State Change successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253333r991575_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000140
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security State Change records events related to changes in the security state, such as startup and shutdown of the system.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          System >> Security State Change - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Security State Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253333
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000140 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security State Change successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security State Change records events related to changes in the security state, such as startup and shutdown of the system.

          V-253334 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security System Extension successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253334r991575_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000150
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.
          Check Text:Security Option "Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings" must be set to "Enabled" (WN11-SO-000030) for the detailed auditing subcategories to be effective.

          Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          System >> Security System Extension - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> System >> "Audit Security System Extension" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253334
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000150 - The system must be configured to audit System - Security System Extension successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Security System Extension records events related to extension code being loaded by the security subsystem.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Security System Extension' is set to 'Success')
              • false ('Audit Security System Extension' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Security System Extension' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)

          • Collect any available items.
