          V-253337 - The Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253337r958752_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000500
          Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Check Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

          If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application\

          Value Name: MaxSize

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00008000 (32768) (or greater)
          Fix Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Application >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "32768" or greater.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253337
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000500 - The Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.
          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '32768'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253338 - The Security event log size must be configured to 1024000 KB or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253338r958752_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000505
          Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Check Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

          If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security\

          Value Name: MaxSize

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x000fa000 (1024000) (or greater)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Security >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "1024000" or greater.

          If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this must be documented with the ISSO.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253338
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000505 - The Security event log size must be configured to 1024000 KB or greater.
          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The Security event log size is configured to 1024000 KB or greater)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The Security event log size is configured to 1024000 KB or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333800 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '1024000'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253339 - The System event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253339r958752_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000510
          Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Check Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

          If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\System\

          Value Name: MaxSize

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00008000 (32768) (or greater)
          Fix Text:If the system is configured to send audit records directly to an audit server, this is NA. This must be documented with the ISSO.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> System >> "Specify the maximum log file size (KB)" to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "32768" or greater.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253339
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000510 - The System event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.
          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The System event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The System event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '32768'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\System\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253344 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253344r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000555
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Policy Change Events contains events about EFS Data Recovery Agent policy changes, changes in Windows Filtering Platform filter, status on Security policy settings updates for local Group Policy settings, Central Access Policy changes, and detailed troubleshooting events for Cryptographic Next Generation (CNG) operations.
          Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Policy Change >> Other Policy Change Events - Failure
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change>> "Audit Other Policy Change Events" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253344
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000555 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Other Policy Change Events Failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Policy Change Events contains events about EFS Data Recovery Agent policy changes, changes in Windows Filtering Platform filter, status on Security policy settings updates for local Group Policy settings, Central Access Policy changes, and detailed troubleshooting events for Cryptographic Next Generation (CNG) operations.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Other Policy Change Events' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Other Policy Change Events' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334400 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_policy_change_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334401 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_policy_change_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • distribution_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_quick_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logoff equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • network_policy_server equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_logon_logoff_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • special_logon equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logon_claims equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_generated equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • detailed_file_share equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • file_share equals 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          • file_system equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          • filtering_platform_packet_drop equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-253345 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253345r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000560
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
          Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Logon/Logoff >> Other Logon/Logoff Events - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Logon/Logoff >> "Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253345
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000560 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit other Logon/Logoff Events Successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Other Logon/Logoff Events determines whether Windows generates audit events for other logon or logoff events. Logon events are essential to understanding user activity and detecting potential attacks.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Logon' is set to 'Failure' and 'Succss and Failure
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Log On' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_logon_logoff_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334501 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Log On' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'other_logon_logoff_events' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          V-253347 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Detailed File Share Failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253347r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000570
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Detailed File Share allows the user to audit attempts to access files and folders on a shared folder.
          The Detailed File Share setting logs an event every time a file or folder is accessed, whereas the File Share setting only records one event for any connection established between a client and file share. Detailed File Share audit events include detailed information about the permissions or other criteria used to grant or deny access.
          Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Object Access >> Detailed File Share - Failure
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Detailed File Share" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253347
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000570 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit Detailed File Share Failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit Detailed File Share allows the user to audit attempts to access files and folders on a shared folder.
          The Detailed File Share setting logs an event every time a file or folder is accessed, whereas the File Share setting only records one event for any connection established between a client and file share. Detailed File Share audit events include detailed information about the permissions or other criteria used to grant or deny access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Detailed File Share' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25334700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Detailed File Share' is set to 'Failure' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'detailed_file_share' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334701 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'detailed_file_share' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-253348 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253348r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000575
          Identities:CCI-000130 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 a)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).
          Check Text:Use the AuditPol tool to review the current Audit Policy configuration:
          Open a Command Prompt with elevated privileges ("Run as Administrator").
          Enter "AuditPol /get /category:*".

          Compare the AuditPol settings with the following. If the system does not audit the following, this is a finding:

          Policy Change >> MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change - Success
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Policy Change >> Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253348
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000575 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change Successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

          Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change determines whether the operating system generates audit events when changes are made to policy rules for the Microsoft Protection Service (MPSSVC.exe).
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure')

          Title:'Audit MPSSVC Rule-Level Policy Change' is set to 'Success' or 'Success and Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334800 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)

          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25334801 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)

          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'mpssvc_rule_level_policy_change' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

          V-253350 - Camera access from the lock screen must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253350r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:camerainstallation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000005
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Enabling camera access from the lock screen could allow for unauthorized use. Requiring logon will ensure the device is only used by authorized personnel.
          Check Text:If the device does not have a camera, this is NA.

          If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\

          Value Name: NoLockScreenCamera

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1
          Fix Text:If the device does not have a camera, this is NA.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Control Panel >> Personalization >> "Prevent enabling lock screen camera" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253350
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Camera access from the lock screen must be disabled.
          Description:Enabling camera access from the lock screen could allow for unauthorized use. Requiring logon will ensure the device is only used by authorized personnel.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Camera access from the lock screen must be disabled.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25335001 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Camera access from the lock screen must be disabled.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25335001 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Prevent enabling lock screen camera
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoLockScreenCamera'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25335001 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value Equal To 1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenCamera'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253357 - Local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253357r958518_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000037
          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
          Description: A compromised local administrator account can provide means for an attacker to move laterally between domain systems.

          With User Account Control enabled, filtering the privileged token for built-in administrator accounts will prevent the elevated privileges of these accounts from being used over the network.
          Check Text:If the system is not a member of a domain, this is NA.

          If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

          Value Name: LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000000 (0)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Apply UAC restrictions to local accounts on network logons" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253357
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000037 - Local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain systems.
          Description:A compromised local administrator account can provide means for an attacker to move laterally between domain systems.

          With User Account Control enabled, filtering the privileged token for built-in administrator accounts will prevent the elevated privileges of these accounts from being used over the network.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Local admin accounts filtered token policy is enabled)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Local admin accounts filtered token policy is enabled
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25335700 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253359 - Run as different user must be removed from context menus.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253359r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000039
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: The "Run as different user" selection from context menus allows the use of credentials other than the currently logged on user. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft. Removing this option from context menus helps prevent this from occurring.
          Check Text:If the following registry values do not exist or are not configured as specified, this is a finding.
          The policy configures the same Value Name, Type and Value under four different registry paths.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Paths:
          \SOFTWARE\Classes\batfile\shell\runasuser\
          \SOFTWARE\Classes\cmdfile\shell\runasuser\
          \SOFTWARE\Classes\exefile\shell\runasuser\
          \SOFTWARE\Classes\mscfile\shell\runasuser\

          Value Name: SuppressionPolicy

          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00001000 (4096)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> "Remove "Run as Different User" from context menus" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and "SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253359
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000039 - Run as different user must be removed from context menus.
          Description:The "Run as different user" selection from context menus allows the use of credentials other than the currently logged on user. Using privileged credentials in a standard user session can expose those credentials to theft. Removing this option from context menus helps prevent this from occurring.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (batfile))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (cmdfile))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (exefile))
              • false ('Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (mscfile))
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Remove 'Run as Different User' from context menus' is set to 'Enabled' (batfile)
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Classes\batfile\shell\runasuser'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SuppressionPolicy'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25335900 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 4096
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '4096'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Classes\batfile\shell\runasuser\SuppressionPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335901 (registry_test)

          V-253360 - Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253360r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000040
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation\

          Value Name: AllowInsecureGuestAuth

          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000000 (0)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Lanman Workstation >> "Enable insecure guest logons" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253360
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000040 - Insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.
          Description:Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowInsecureGuestAuth'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation\AllowInsecureGuestAuth'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253362 - Hardened UNC Paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253362r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000050
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Additional security requirements are applied to Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths specified in Hardened UNC paths before allowing access them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
          Check Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          If the following registry values do not exist or are not configured as specified, this is a finding.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths\

          Value Name: \\*\NETLOGON
          Value Type: REG_SZ
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1

          Value Name: \\*\SYSVOL
          Value Type: REG_SZ
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1

          Additional entries would not be a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Network Provider >> "Hardened UNC Paths" to "Enabled" with at least the following configured in "Hardened UNC Paths:" (click the "Show" button to display).

          Value Name: \\*\SYSVOL
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1

          Value Name: \\*\NETLOGON
          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253362
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000050 - Hardened UNC Paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares.
          Description:Additional security requirements are applied to Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths specified in Hardened UNC paths before allowing access them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL)
              • false (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336200 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336200 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\SYSVOL
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '\\*\SYSVOL'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireIntegrity=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireIntegrity!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths\\\*\SYSVOL'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336201 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336201 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\NETLOGON
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to '\\*\NETLOGON'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)
          State Comment:RequireIntegrity=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=1( *, *|$)'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)
          State Comment:No RequireIntegrity!=1
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = no 'value' can match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=(?!1( *, *|$))'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths\\\*\NETLOGON'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253363 - Windows 11 must be configured to prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253363r971535_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000052
          Identities:CCI-000803 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-7.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-7; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-7)
          Description: Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data. By default Windows uses ECC curves with shorter key lengths first. Requiring ECC curves with longer key lengths to be prioritized first helps ensure more secure algorithms are used.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography\Configuration\SSL\00010002\

          Value Name: EccCurves

          Value Type: REG_MULTI_SZ
          Value: NistP384 NistP256
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> SSL Configuration Settings >> "ECC Curve Order" to "Enabled" with "ECC Curve Order:" including the following in the order listed:

          NistP384
          NistP256
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253363
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Windows 11 must be configured to prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first.
          Description:Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data. By default Windows uses ECC curves with shorter key lengths first. Requiring ECC curves with longer key lengths to be prioritized first helps ensure more secure algorithms are used.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first. NistP384 Requirement)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25336301 (variable_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Prioritize ECC Curves with longer key lengths first. NistP384 Requirement
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25336301 (variable_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'var_ref' must be equal to 'oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:var:25336301'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25336301 (variable_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern '^NistP384\s*NistP256$'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • var_ref equals 'oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:var:25336301'
          • value equals ''
          Result Analysis:'' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to '^NistP384\s*NistP256$'

          V-253365 - Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253365r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000060
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Multiple network connections can provide additional attack vectors to a system and must be limited. When connected to a domain, communication must go through the domain connection.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\

          Value Name: fBlockNonDomain

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Windows Connection Manager >> "Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253365
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000060 - Connections to non-domain networks when connected to a domain authenticated network must be blocked.
          Description:Multiple network connections can provide additional attack vectors to a system and must be limited. When connected to a domain, communication must go through the domain connection.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336500 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Prohibit connection to non-domain networks when connected to domain authenticated network' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'fBlockNonDomain'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WcmSvc\GroupPolicy\\fBlockNonDomain'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253368 - Windows 11 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253368r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000068
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\

          Value Name: AllowProtectedCreds

          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000001 (1)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Credentials Delegation >> "Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253368
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000068 - Windows 11 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials.
          Description:An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of non-exportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowProtectedCreds'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\AllowProtectedCreds'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253369 - Virtualization-based Security must be enabled on Windows 11 with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253369r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000070
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Virtualization-based Security (VBS) provides the platform for the additional security features, Credential Guard and virtualization-based protection of code integrity. Secure Boot is the minimum security level with DMA protection providing additional memory protection. DMA Protection requires a CPU that supports input/output memory management unit (IOMMU).
          Check Text:Confirm virtualization-based Security is enabled and running with Secure Boot or Secure Boot and DMA Protection.

          For those devices that support virtualization-based security (VBS) features, including Credential Guard or protection of code integrity, this must be enabled. If the system meets the hardware and firmware dependencies for enabling VBS but it is not enabled, this is a CAT III finding.

          Virtualization-based security, including Credential Guard, currently cannot be implemented in virtual desktop implementations (VDI) due to specific supporting requirements including a TPM, UEFI with Secure Boot, and the capability to run the Hyper-V feature within the virtual desktop.

          For VDIs where the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

          Run "PowerShell" with elevated privileges (run as administrator).

          Enter the following:

          "Get-CimInstance -ClassName Win32_DeviceGuard -Namespace root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard"

          If "RequiredSecurityProperties" does not include a value of "2" indicating "Secure Boot" (e.g., "{1, 2}"), this is a finding.

          If "Secure Boot and DMA Protection" is configured, "3" will also be displayed in the results (e.g., "{1, 2, 3}").

          If "VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus" is not a value of "2" indicating "Running", this is a finding.

          Alternately:

          Run "System Information".

          Under "System Summary", verify the following:

          If "Device Guard virtualization-based security" does not display "Running", this is finding.

          If "Device Guard Required Security Properties" does not display "Base Virtualization Support, Secure Boot", this is finding.

          If "Secure Boot and DMA Protection" is configured, "DMA Protection" will also be displayed (e.g., "Base Virtualization Support, Secure Boot, DMA Protection").

          The policy settings referenced in the Fix section will configure the following registry values. However due to hardware requirements, the registry values alone do not ensure proper function.

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard\

          Value Name: EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity
          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1

          Value Name: RequirePlatformSecurityFeatures
          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1 (Secure Boot only) or 3 (Secure Boot and DMA Protection)

          A Microsoft article on Credential Guard system requirement can be found at the following link:

          https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/itpro/windows/keep-secure/credential-guard-requirements
          Fix Text:Virtualization-based security, including Credential Guard, currently cannot be implemented in virtual desktop implementations (VDI) due to specific supporting requirements including a TPM, UEFI with Secure Boot, and the capability to run the Hyper-V feature within the virtual desktop.

          For VDIs where the virtual desktop instance is deleted or refreshed upon logoff, this is NA.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Device Guard >> "Turn On virtualization-based Security" to "Enabled" with "Secure Boot" or "Secure Boot and DMA Protection" selected for "Select Platform Security Level:".

          A Microsoft article on Credential Guard system requirement can be found at the following link.
          https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/itpro/windows/keep-secure/credential-guard-requirements
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253369
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Virtualization-based Security must be enabled on Windows 11 with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.
          Description:Virtualization-based Security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (RequiredSecurityProperties should return a value of 2)
            • true (VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus should return a value of 2)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25336901 (cmdlet_test)
          Result:false
          Title:RequiredSecurityProperties should return a value of 2
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25336901 (cmdlet_object)
          Object Comment:DeviceGuard requiredsecurityproperties
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'module_name' must be equal to 'CimCmdlets'
          • check = 'verb' must be equal to 'Get'
          • check = 'noun' must be equal to 'CimInstance'
          • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
            • classname must be equal to 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
            • namespace must be equal to 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
          • for 'select' the following must be true:
            • property must be equal to 'requiredsecurityproperties'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25336901 (cmdlet_state)
          State Comment:requiredsecurityproperties must match a pattern of 2
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'value' the following must be true:
            • all requiredsecurityproperties must match the pattern '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • module_name equals 'CimCmdlets'
          • module_id equals '{fb6cc51d-c096-4b38-b78d-0fed6277096a}'
          • module_version equals '1.0.0.0'
          • verb equals 'Get'
          • noun equals 'CimInstance'
          • collected 'parameters' result:
            • classname = 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
            • namespace = 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
          • collected 'select' result:
            • property = 'requiredsecurityproperties'
          • collected 'value' result:
            • requiredsecurityproperties = '{1}'
          Result Analysis:requiredsecurityproperties = '{1}' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to '2'
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25336902 (cmdlet_test)
          Result:true
          Title:VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus should return a value of 2
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25336902 (cmdlet_object)
          Object Comment:DeviceGuard virtualizationbasedsecuritystatus
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'module_name' must be equal to 'CimCmdlets'
          • check = 'verb' must be equal to 'Get'
          • check = 'noun' must be equal to 'CimInstance'
          • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
            • classname must be equal to 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
            • namespace must be equal to 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
          • for 'select' the following must be true:
            • property must be equal to 'virtualizationbasedsecuritystatus'

          State Comment:requiredsecurityproperties must match a pattern of 2
          State Requirements:
