          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253435 - The built-in administrator account must be renamed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253435r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000020
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The built-in administrator account is a well-known account subject to attack. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options.

          If the value for "Accounts: Rename administrator account" is set to "Administrator", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Accounts: Rename administrator account" to a name other than "Administrator".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253435
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000020 - The built-in administrator account must be renamed.
          Description:The built-in administrator account is a well-known account subject to attack. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Accounts: Rename administrator account' is not set to 'Administrator' or 'localdomain\Administrator')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343500 (sid_sid_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Accounts: Rename administrator account' is not set to 'Administrator' or 'localdomain\Administrator'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343500 (sid_sid_object)
          Object Comment:Built-in Administrator SID
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'trustee_sid' must match the pattern '^S-1-5-[0-9-]+-500$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343500 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not Administrator
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'trustee_name' must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'Administrator'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343501 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not localdomain\Administrator
          State Requirements:
          • for alltrustee_name, the following must be true:
            • trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'MHL-PW06M2V2\Administrator'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-2056937484-830314662-3505637335-500'
          • trustee_name equals 'Administrator'
          • trustee_domain equals 'MHL-PW06M2V2'
          Result Analysis:'Administrator' failed test of 'case insensitive not equal' compared to 'Administrator'

          V-253436 - The built-in guest account must be renamed.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253436r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000025
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: The built-in guest account is a well-known user account on all Windows systems and, as initially installed, does not require a password. This can allow access to system resources by unauthorized users. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options.

          If the value for "Accounts: Rename guest account" is set to "Guest", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Accounts: Rename guest account" to a name other than "Guest".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253436
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000025 - The built-in guest account must be renamed.
          Description:The built-in guest account is a well-known user account on all Windows systems and, as initially installed, does not require a password. This can allow access to system resources by unauthorized users. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Accounts: Rename Guest account' is not set to 'Guest' or 'localdomain\Guest')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343600 (sid_sid_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Accounts: Rename Guest account' is not set to 'Guest' or 'localdomain\Guest'
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343600 (sid_sid_object)
          Object Comment:Built-in Guest SID
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'trustee_sid' must match the pattern '^S-1-5-[0-9-]+-501$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343600 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not Guest
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'trustee_name' must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guest'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343601 (sid_sid_state)
          State Comment:Not localdomain\Guest
          State Requirements:
          • for alltrustee_name, the following must be true:
            • trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'MHL-PW06M2V2\Guest'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-2056937484-830314662-3505637335-501'
          • trustee_name equals 'Guest'
          • trustee_domain equals 'MHL-PW06M2V2'
          Result Analysis:'Guest' failed test of 'case insensitive not equal' compared to 'Guest'

          V-253444 - The machine inactivity limit must be set to 15 minutes, locking the system with the screensaver.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253444r958636_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000070
          Identities:CCI-001133 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-10; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-10.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-10; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-10)
          CCI-002361 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-12; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-12)
          Description: Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and must be locked when unattended. The screen saver must be set at a maximum of 15 minutes and be password protected. This protects critical and sensitive data from exposure to unauthorized personnel with physical access to the computer.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000279-GPOS-00109, SRG-OS-000163-GPOS-00072
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

          Value Name: InactivityTimeoutSecs

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000384 (900) (or less, excluding "0" which is effectively disabled)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Interactive logon: Machine inactivity limit" to "900" seconds" or less, excluding "0" which is effectively disabled.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253444
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000070 - The machine inactivity limit must be set to 15 minutes, locking the system with the screensaver.
          Description:Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and must be locked when unattended. The screen saver must be set at a maximum of 15 minutes and be password protected. This protects critical and sensitive data from exposure to unauthorized personnel with physical access to the computer.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000279-GPOS-00109, SRG-OS-000163-GPOS-00072
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The machine inactivity limit is set to 15 minutes or less)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:tst:25344400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The machine inactivity limit is set to 15 minutes or less
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:obj:25344400 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal (case insensitive) to 'InactivityTimeoutSecs'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:ste:25344400 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be less than or equal to '900'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:ste:25344401 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\InactivityTimeoutSecs'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253445 - The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253445r958392_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000075
          Identities:CCI-000044 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-7 a)
          CCI-000048 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 a)
          CCI-000050 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-8 b; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-8.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-8 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-8 b)
          Description: Failure to display the logon banner prior to a logon attempt will negate legal proceedings resulting from unauthorized access to system resources.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000024-GPOS-00007, SRG-OS-000228-GPOS-00088, SRG-OS-000024-GPOS-00007
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

          Value Name: LegalNoticeText

          Value Type: REG_SZ
          Value:
          You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.

          By using this IS (which includes any device attached to this IS), you consent to the following conditions:

          -The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct (PM), law enforcement (LE), and counterintelligence (CI) investigations.

          -At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS.

          -Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose.

          -This IS includes security measures (e.g., authentication and access controls) to protect USG interests--not for your personal benefit or privacy.

          -Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Interactive logon: Message text for users attempting to log on" to the following.

          You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.

          By using this IS (which includes any device attached to this IS), you consent to the following conditions:

          -The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations and defense, personnel misconduct (PM), law enforcement (LE), and counterintelligence (CI) investigations.

          -At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS.

          -Communications using, or data stored on, this IS are not private, are subject to routine monitoring, interception, and search, and may be disclosed or used for any USG-authorized purpose.

          -This IS includes security measures (e.g., authentication and access controls) to protect USG interests--not for your personal benefit or privacy.

          -Notwithstanding the above, using this IS does not constitute consent to PM, LE or CI investigative searching or monitoring of the content of privileged communications, or work product, related to personal representation or services by attorneys, psychotherapists, or clergy, and their assistants. Such communications and work product are private and confidential. See User Agreement for details.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253445
          Definition Result:false
          Title:The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon.
          Description:Failure to display the logon banner prior to a logon attempt will negate legal proceedings resulting from unauthorized access to system resources.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 1)
            • false (The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 2)
            • false (The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 3)
            • false (The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 4)
            • false (The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 5)
            • false (The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 6)
            • false (The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 7)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25344501 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The required legal notice must be configured to display before console logon - Message Content 1
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25344501 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Legal notice text
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'LegalNoticeText'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25344501 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Warning Banner - You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must match the pattern 'You are accessing a U.S. Government \(USG\) Information System \(IS\) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • name equals 'LegalNoticeText'
          • last_write_time equals '134129782100000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals 'Communications and files transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, including networks, computers, e-mail and voice mail systems, and any back-up copies of the Company's Electronic Resources, whether for business or non-business reason, are not private or confidential from the perspective of the Company., The Company regularly monitors the content of e-mails, voice mails, voice communications, and other communications transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, regardless of whether the communication is for business or non-business purposes. The Company monitors e-mail and instant messages using software designed to capture the content of any e-mail or instant message as it passes through the Company's servers., By using these Electronic Resources, you are consenting to the Company's monitoring of your use of Electronic Resources, including, without limitation, the content of your voice communications, e-mails, instant messages, and other communications, regardless of whether those communications are for business or non-business reasons.'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\LegalNoticeText\value = 'Communications and files transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, including networks, computers, e-mail and voice mail systems, and any back-up copies of the Company's Electronic Resources, whether for business or non-business reason, are not private or confidential from the perspective of the Company., The Company regularly monitors the content of e-mails, voice mails, voice communications, and other communications transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, regardless of whether the communication is for business or non-business purposes. The Company monitors e-mail and instant messages using software designed to capture the content of any e-mail or instant message as it passes through the Company's servers., By using these Electronic Resources, you are consenting to the Company's monitoring of your use of Electronic Resources, including, without limitation, the content of your voice communications, e-mails, instant messages, and other communications, regardless of whether those communications are for business or non-business reasons.' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to 'You are accessing a U.S. Government \(USG\) Information System \(IS\) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.'

          • value equals 'Communications and files transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, including networks, computers, e-mail and voice mail systems, and any back-up copies of the Company's Electronic Resources, whether for business or non-business reason, are not private or confidential from the perspective of the Company., The Company regularly monitors the content of e-mails, voice mails, voice communications, and other communications transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, regardless of whether the communication is for business or non-business purposes. The Company monitors e-mail and instant messages using software designed to capture the content of any e-mail or instant message as it passes through the Company's servers., By using these Electronic Resources, you are consenting to the Company's monitoring of your use of Electronic Resources, including, without limitation, the content of your voice communications, e-mails, instant messages, and other communications, regardless of whether those communications are for business or non-business reasons.'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\LegalNoticeText\value = 'Communications and files transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, including networks, computers, e-mail and voice mail systems, and any back-up copies of the Company's Electronic Resources, whether for business or non-business reason, are not private or confidential from the perspective of the Company., The Company regularly monitors the content of e-mails, voice mails, voice communications, and other communications transmitted over, or stored on, the Company's Electronic Resources, regardless of whether the communication is for business or non-business purposes. The Company monitors e-mail and instant messages using software designed to capture the content of any e-mail or instant message as it passes through the Company's servers., By using these Electronic Resources, you are consenting to the Company's monitoring of your use of Electronic Resources, including, without limitation, the content of your voice communications, e-mails, instant messages, and other communications, regardless of whether those communications are for business or non-business reasons.' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to '-At any time, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS.'

          V-253448 - The Smart Card removal option must be configured to Force Logoff or Lock Workstation.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253448r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000095
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and must be locked. Configuring a system to lock when a smart card is removed will ensure the system is inaccessible when unattended.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\

          Value Name: SCRemoveOption

          Value Type: REG_SZ
          Value: 1 (Lock Workstation) or 2 (Force Logoff)

          This can be left not configured or set to "No action" on workstations with the following conditions. This must be documented with the ISSO.
          -The setting cannot be configured due to mission needs, or because it interferes with applications.
          -Policy must be in place that users manually lock workstations when leaving them unattended.
          -The screen saver is properly configured to lock as required.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Interactive logon: Smart card removal behavior" to "Lock Workstation" or "Force Logoff".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253448
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000095 - The Smart Card removal option must be configured to Force Logoff or Lock Workstation.
          Description:Unattended systems are susceptible to unauthorized use and must be locked. Configuring a system to lock when a smart card is removed will ensure the system is inaccessible when unattended.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Interactive logon: Smart card removal behavior' is set to 'Lock Workstation' or 'Force Logoff')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25344800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Interactive logon: Smart card removal behavior' is set to 'Lock Workstation' or 'Force Logoff'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25344800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'SCRemoveOption'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25445900 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25445901 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
          • name equals 'SCRemoveOption'
          • last_write_time equals '134188486430000000'
          • type equals 'reg_sz'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\SCRemoveOption\value = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '2'
          HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\SCRemoveOption\value = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

          V-253457 - Remote calls to the Security Account Manager (SAM) must be restricted to Administrators.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253457r1081060_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000167
          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: The Windows SAM stores users' passwords. Restricting remote rpc connections to the SAM to Administrators helps protect those credentials.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\

          Value Name: RestrictRemoteSAM

          Value Type: REG_SZ
          Value: O:BAG:BAD:(A;;RC;;;BA)

          If a domain application account such as for a management tool requires this user right, this would not be a finding.
          Vendor documentation must support the requirement for having the user right.
          The requirement must be documented with the ISSO.
          Fix Text:Navigate to the policy Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network access: Restrict clients allowed to make remote calls to SAM".

          Select "Edit Security" to configure the "Security descriptor:".

          Add "Administrators" in "Group or user names:" if it is not already listed (this is the default).

          Select "Administrators" in "Group or user names:".

          Select "Allow" for "Remote Access" in "Permissions for "Administrators".

          Click "OK".

          The "Security descriptor:" must be populated with "O:BAG:BAD:(A;;RC;;;BA) for the policy to be enforced.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253457
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000167 - Remote calls to the Security Account Manager (SAM) must be restricted to Administrators.
          Description:The Windows Security Account Manager (SAM) stores users' passwords. Restricting remote rpc connections to the SAM to Administrators helps protect those credentials.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Allow Administrators group to make remote calls to SAM)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:tst:25345700 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Allow Administrators group to make remote calls to SAM
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:obj:25345700 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'RestrictRemoteSAM'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:ste:25345700 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Reg_Sz type and value equals O:BAG:BAD:(A;;RC;;;BA)
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_sz'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to 'O:BAG:BAD:(A;;RC;;;BA)'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\RestrictRemoteSAM'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253458 - NTLM must be prevented from falling back to a Null session.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253458r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000180
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: NTLM sessions that are allowed to fall back to Null (unauthenticated) sessions may gain unauthorized access.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\MSV1_0\

          Value Name: allownullsessionfallback

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Allow LocalSystem NULL session fallback" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253458
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000180 - NTLM must be prevented from falling back to a Null session.
          Description:NTLM sessions that are allowed to fall back to Null (unauthenticated) sessions may gain unauthorized access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Network security: Allow LocalSystem NULL session fallback' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345800 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Network security: Allow LocalSystem NULL session fallback' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345800 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\MSV1_0'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'allownullsessionfallback'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\MSV1_0\allownullsessionfallback'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253459 - PKU2U authentication using online identities must be prevented.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253459r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000185
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: PKU2U is a peer-to-peer authentication protocol. This setting prevents online identities from authenticating to domain-joined systems. Authentication will be centrally managed with Windows user accounts.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\pku2u\

          Value Name: AllowOnlineID

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Allow PKU2U authentication requests to this computer to use online identities" to "Disabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253459
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000185 - PKU2U authentication using online identities must be prevented.
          Description:PKU2U is a peer-to-peer authentication protocol. This setting prevents online identities from authenticating to domain-joined systems. Authentication will be centrally managed with Windows user accounts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Network Security: Allow PKU2U authentication requests to this computer to use online identities' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25345900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Network Security: Allow PKU2U authentication requests to this computer to use online identities' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25345900 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Check the registry value at HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\pku2u\AllowOnlineID
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\pku2u'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'AllowOnlineID'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment:

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\LSA\pku2u\AllowOnlineID'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253464 - The system must be configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253464r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000215
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Microsoft has implemented a variety of security support providers for use with RPC sessions. All of the options must be enabled to ensure the maximum security level.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\MSV1_0\

          Value Name: NTLMMinClientSec

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x20080000 (537395200)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "Network security: Minimum session security for NTLM SSP based (including secure RPC) clients" to "Require NTLMv2 session security" and "Require 128-bit encryption" (all options selected).
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253464
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000215 - The system must be configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.
          Description:Microsoft has implemented a variety of security support providers for use with RPC sessions. All of the options must be enabled to ensure the maximum security level.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The system is configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The system is configured to meet the minimum session security requirement for NTLM SSP based clients.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\MSV1_0'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NTLMMinClientSec'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25346500 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Value bitwise and 537395200
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '537395200'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          V-253466 - The system must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253466r1137699_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000230
          Identities:CCI-002450 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-13; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-13 b)
          Description: This setting ensures that the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy\

          Value Name: Enabled

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1

          Warning: Clients with this setting enabled will not be able to communicate via digitally encrypted or signed protocols with servers that do not support these algorithms.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing" to "Enabled".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253466
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000230 - The system must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.
          Description:This setting ensures that the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'Enabled'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
          • name equals 'Enabled'
          • last_write_time equals '134130564080000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '0'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy\Enabled\value = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

          V-253469 - User Account Control must prompt administrators for consent on the secure desktop.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253469r958518_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000250
          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
          Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the elevation requirements for logged on administrators to complete a task that requires raised privileges.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

          Value Name: ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 2 (Prompt for consent on the secure desktop)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode" to "Prompt for consent on the secure desktop".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253469
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000250 - User Account Control must prompt administrators for consent on the secure desktop.
          Description:User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the elevation requirements for logged on administrators to complete a task that requires raised privileges.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Prompt for consent on the secure desktop')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346900 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Prompt for consent on the secure desktop'

          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)

          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'

          V-253470 - Windows 11 must use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged and nonprivileged accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253470r1106510_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000251
          Identities:CCI-000765 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-2 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-2 (1).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (1))
          Description: Without the use of multifactor authentication, the ease of access to privileged and nonprivileged functions is greatly increased.

          All domain accounts must be enabled for multifactor authentication with the exception of local emergency accounts.

          Multifactor authentication requires using two or more factors to achieve authentication.

          Factors include:

          1) Something a user knows (e.g., password/PIN);

          2) Something a user has (e.g., cryptographic identification device, token); and

          3) Something a user is (e.g., biometric).

          A privileged account is defined as an information system account with authorizations of a privileged user.

          Network access is defined as access to an information system by a user (or a process acting on behalf of a user) communicating through a network (e.g., local area network, wide area network, or the internet).

          Local access is defined as access to an organizational information system by a user (or process acting on behalf of a user) communicating through a direct connection without the use of a network.

          The DoD CAC with DoD-approved PKI is an example of multifactor authentication.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000106-GPOS-00053, SRG-OS-000107-GPOS-00054, SRG-OS-000108-GPOS-00055
          Check Text:If the system is not a member of a domain, this is Not Applicable.

          If all of the following settings exist and are populated, this is not a finding.

          \HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\Readers
          \HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\SmartCards
          Fix Text:For nondomain joined systems, configuring Windows Hello for sign-on options would be suggested based on the organization's needs and capabilities.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253470
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000251 - Windows 11 must use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.
          Description:Without the use of multifactor authentication, the ease of access to privileged and non-privileged functions is greatly increased.

          All domain accounts must be enabled for multifactor authentication with the exception of local emergency accounts.

          Multifactor authentication requires using two or more factors to achieve authentication.

          Factors include:

          1) Something a user knows (e.g., password/PIN);

          2) Something a user has (e.g., cryptographic identification device, token); and

          3) Something a user is (e.g., biometric).

          A privileged account is defined as an information system account with authorizations of a privileged user.

          Network access is defined as access to an information system by a user (or a process acting on behalf of a user) communicating through a network (e.g., local area network, wide area network, or the Internet).

          Local access is defined as access to an organizational information system by a user (or process acting on behalf of a user) communicating through a direct connection without the use of a network.

          The DoD CAC with DoD-approved PKI is an example of multifactor authentication.

          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000106-GPOS-00053, SRG-OS-000107-GPOS-00054, SRG-OS-000108-GPOS-00055
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\Readers exists and contains at least one key.)
              • true (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\SmartCards exists and contains at least one key.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347000 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\Readers exists and contains at least one key.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347000 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must match the pattern 'SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Cryptography\\Calais\\Readers\\\S+'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Cryptography\\Calais\\Readers\\\S+\'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347001 (registry_test)
          Result:true
          Title:HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\SmartCards exists and contains at least one key.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347001 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must match the pattern 'SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Cryptography\\Calais\\SmartCards\\\S+'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ exists ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\SmartCards\Identity Device (Microsoft Generic Profile)'
          • last_write_time equals '-1'
          • type does not exist
          • value does not exist
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ exists ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\Calais\SmartCards\Identity Device (NIST SP 800-73 [PIV])'
          • last_write_time equals '-1'
          • type does not exist
          • value does not exist

          V-253471 - User Account Control must automatically deny elevation requests for standard users.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253471r1051058_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000255
          Identities:CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-11)
          Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. Denying elevation requests from standard user accounts requires tasks that need elevation to be initiated by accounts with administrative privileges. This ensures correct accounts are used on the system for privileged tasks to help mitigate credential theft.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

          Value Name: ConsentPromptBehaviorUser

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> "User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users" to "Automatically deny elevation requests".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253471
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-SO-000255 - User Account Control must automatically deny elevation requests for standard users.
          Description:User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. Denying elevation requests from standard user accounts requires tasks that need elevation to be initiated by accounts with administrative privileges. This ensures correct accounts are used on the system for privileged tasks to help mitigate credential theft.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users' is set to 'Automatically deny elevation requests')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347100 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users' is set to 'Automatically deny elevation requests'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347100 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'ConsentPromptBehaviorUser'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • key equals 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
          • name equals 'ConsentPromptBehaviorUser'
          • last_write_time equals '134129782100000000'
          • type equals 'reg_dword'
          • value equals '3'
          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\ConsentPromptBehaviorUser\value = '3' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          V-253476 - Passwords for enabled local Administrator accounts must be changed at least every 60 days.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253476r1051060_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:local_enabled_administrators
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-SO-000280
          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
          CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
          Description: The longer a password is in use, the greater the opportunity for someone to gain unauthorized knowledge of the password. A local Administrator account is not generally used and its password may not be changed as frequently as necessary. Changing the password for enabled Administrator accounts on a regular basis will limit its exposure.

          Windows LAPS must be used to change the built-in Administrator account password.
          Check Text:If there are no enabled local Administrator accounts, this is Not Applicable.

          Review the password last set date for the enabled local Administrator account.

          On the standalone or domain-joined workstation:

          Open "PowerShell".

          Enter "Get-LocalUser -Name * | Select-Object *".

          If the "PasswordLastSet" date is greater than "60" days old for the local Administrator account for administering the computer/domain, this is a finding.

          Verify LAPS is configured and operational.

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> LAPS >> Password Settings >> Set to enabled. Password Complexity, large letters + small letters + numbers + special, Password Length 14, Password Age 60. If not configured as shown, this is a finding.

          Verify LAPS Operational logs >> Event Viewer >> Applications and Services Logs >> Microsoft >> Windows >> LAPS >> Operational. Verify LAPS policy process is completing. If it is not, this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Change the enabled local Administrator account password at least every 60 days.

          Windows LAPS must be used to change the built-in Administrator account password. Domain-joined and nondomain-joined systems can configure this to occur more frequently. LAPS will change the password every 30 days by default.

          More information is available at:
          https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/windows-it-pro-blog/by-popular-demand-windows-laps-available-now/ba-p/3788747
          https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/laps/laps-overview#windows-laps-supported-platforms-and-azure-ad-laps-preview-status
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:def:253476
          Definition Result:false
          Title:Passwords for enabled local Administrator accounts must be changed at least every 60 days.
          Description:Windows Server passwords for the built-in Administrator account must be changed at least every 60 days.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Password Age Check: Verify all enabled local Administrator accounts passwords are less than 60 days old)
            • false (LAPS Compliance: Password Age - 60)
            • false (LAPS Compliance: Password Length - 14)
            • false (LAPS Compliance: Password Complexity - large letters + small letters + numbers + specials)
            • true (Verify LAPS policy process is completing)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347601 (user_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Password Age Check: Verify all enabled local Administrator accounts passwords are less than 60 days old
          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347601 (user_object)
          Object Comment:Enabled Local Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'user' must match the pattern '.*'
          Include Items If:
          • group equals 'Administrators'
          Include Items If:
          • enabled equals 'true'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347601 (user_state)
          State Comment:Confirm that all enabled local Administrators have a password age less than or equal to 60 days
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'password_age_days' must be less than or equal to '60'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • user equals 'MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService'
          • enabled equals 'true'
          • group equals 'Administrators'
          • last_logon equals '0'
          • full_name equals 'DCProtectService'
          • comment equals ''
          • password_age_days equals '103'
          Result Analysis:MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService = '103' failed test of 'less than or equal' compared to '60'
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347602 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:LAPS Compliance: Password Age - 60
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347602 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Windows LAPS PasswordAgeDays
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'PasswordAgeDays'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347602 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Registry value must be of type reg_dword and value less than or equals 60
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be less than or equal to '60'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS\PasswordAgeDays'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347603 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:LAPS Compliance: Password Length - 14
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347603 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Windows LAPS PasswordLength
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'PasswordLength'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347603 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Registry value must be of type reg_dword and value greater than or equals 14
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be greater than or equal to '14'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS\PasswordLength'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347604 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:LAPS Compliance: Password Complexity - large letters + small letters + numbers + specials
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347604 (registry_object)
          Object Comment:Windows LAPS PasswordComplexity
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'PasswordComplexity'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347604 (registry_state)
          State Comment:Registry value must be of type reg_dword and value equals 4
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '4'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\LAPS\PasswordComplexity'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:tst:25347605 (shellcommand_test)
          Result:true
          Title:Verify LAPS policy process is completing
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:obj:25347605 (shellcommand_object)
          Object Comment:Operational logs - Event Viewer - Applications and Services Logs - Microsoft - Windows - LAPS - Operational
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'shell' must be equal to 'powershell'
          • check = 'command' must be equal to 'get-winevent -logname Microsoft-Windows-LAPS/Operational -MaxEvents 50'
          • check = 'pattern' must be equal to 'LAPS policy processing (\w+)\.'
          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows:ste:25347605 (shellcommand_state)
          State Comment:LAPS Operational
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'shell' must be equal to 'powershell'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'command' must be equal to 'get-winevent -logname Microsoft-Windows-LAPS/Operational -MaxEvents 50'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'subexpression' must match the pattern 'succeeded'

          V-253480 - The "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253480r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000010
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and must be limited to those that require it.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Access this computer from the network" user right, this is a finding:

          Administrators
          Remote Desktop Users

          If a domain application account such as for a management tool requires this user right, this would not be a finding.

          Vendor documentation must support the requirement for having the user right.

          The requirement must be documented with the ISSO.

          The application account, managed at the domain level, must meet requirements for application account passwords, such as length and frequency of changes as defined in the Windows Server STIGs.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Access this computer from the network" to only include the following groups or accounts:

          Administrators
          Remote Desktop Users
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253480
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000010 - The "Access this computer from the network" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and must be limited to those that require it.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The 'Access this computer from the network' user right is only assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348000 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The 'Access this computer from the network' user right is only assigned to the Administrators and Remote Desktop Users groups.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000010 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators, Remote Desktop Users
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Remote Desktop Users'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348000 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'senetworklogonright' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • senetworklogonright equals '1' for: Everyone, Users, Backup Operators
          • senetworklogonright equals '0' for RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, Administrator, Hyper-V Administrators, Access Control Assistance Operators, Power Users, Guests, Event Log Readers, IUSR, SYSTEM, User Mode Hardware Operators, DIALUP, DefaultAccount, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, NETWORK, NETWORK SERVICE, MHL-PW06M2V2\Offer Remote Assistance Helpers, INTERACTIVE, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, RESTRICTED SERVICES\PrintSpoolerService, Distributed COM Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR GROUP, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, OpenSSH Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, WDAGUtilityAccount, Guest, Network Configuration Operators, MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService, Replicator, Remote Management Users, IIS_IUSRS, Performance Monitor Users, System Managed Accounts Group, TERMINAL SERVER USER, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER, MHL-PW06M2V2\ConfigMgr Remote Control Users, LOCAL SERVICE, PROXY, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Authenticated Users, Performance Log Users, Device Owners
          Result Analysis:senetworklogonright : Everyone = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
          senetworklogonright : Users = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
          senetworklogonright : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          V-253482 - The "Allow log on locally" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Users groups.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253482r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000025
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Allow log on locally" user right can log on interactively to a system.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Allow log on locally" user right, this is a finding:

          Administrators
          Users
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Allow log on locally" to only include the following groups or accounts:

          Administrators
          Users
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253482
          Definition Result:false
