          Accounts with the "Allow log on locally" user right can log on interactively to a system.
          Class:compliance

          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))

          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Users'

          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'seinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '0'

          • seinteractivelogonright equals '0' for RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, Everyone, Administrator, Hyper-V Administrators, Access Control Assistance Operators, Power Users, Guests, Event Log Readers, IUSR, SYSTEM, User Mode Hardware Operators, DIALUP, DefaultAccount, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, NETWORK, NETWORK SERVICE, MHL-PW06M2V2\Offer Remote Assistance Helpers, INTERACTIVE, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, RESTRICTED SERVICES\PrintSpoolerService, Distributed COM Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR GROUP, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, OpenSSH Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, WDAGUtilityAccount, Network Configuration Operators, Remote Desktop Users, MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService, Replicator, Remote Management Users, IIS_IUSRS, Performance Monitor Users, System Managed Accounts Group, TERMINAL SERVER USER, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER, MHL-PW06M2V2\ConfigMgr Remote Control Users, LOCAL SERVICE, PROXY, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Authenticated Users, Performance Log Users, Device Owners

          seinteractivelogonright : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          V-253483 - The "Back up files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253483r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000030
          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Back up files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data."
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Back up files and directories" user right, this is a finding:

          Administrators
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Back up files and directories" to only include the following groups or accounts:

          Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0

          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253483
          Definition Result:false

          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Back up files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data."
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Back up files and directories - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348300 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false

          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348300 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Back up files and directories
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sebackupprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sebackupprivilege equals '1' for: MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService, Backup Operators
          • sebackupprivilege equals '0' for RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, Everyone, Administrator, Hyper-V Administrators, Access Control Assistance Operators, Power Users, Guests, Event Log Readers, IUSR, SYSTEM, User Mode Hardware Operators, DIALUP, DefaultAccount, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, NETWORK, NETWORK SERVICE, MHL-PW06M2V2\Offer Remote Assistance Helpers, INTERACTIVE, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, RESTRICTED SERVICES\PrintSpoolerService, Distributed COM Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR GROUP, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, OpenSSH Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, WDAGUtilityAccount, Guest, Network Configuration Operators, Remote Desktop Users, Replicator, Remote Management Users, IIS_IUSRS, Performance Monitor Users, System Managed Accounts Group, TERMINAL SERVER USER, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER, MHL-PW06M2V2\ConfigMgr Remote Control Users, LOCAL SERVICE, Users, PROXY, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Authenticated Users, Performance Log Users, Device Owners
          Result Analysis:sebackupprivilege : MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
          sebackupprivilege : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          V-253491 - The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253491r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000070
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny access to this computer from the network" right, this is a finding:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins group
          Domain Admins group
          Local account (see Note below)

          All Systems:
          Guests group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny access to this computer from the network" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins group
          Domain Admins group
          Local account (see Note below)

          All Systems:
          Guests group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253491
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000070 - The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins)
                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins)
                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account)
                  • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • true (The system is a Windows Domain Member Workstation)
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation)
              • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349101 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349102 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349103 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000007 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenynetworklogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          Object Comment:

          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'

          V-253492 - The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253492r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000075
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a batch job" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.
          Check Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny log on as a batch job" right, this is a finding:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admin Group
          Domain Admin Group
          Fix Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a batch job" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admin Group
          Domain Admin Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253492
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000075 - The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right on domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a batch job" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Domain Admin groups.)
              • false (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Enterprise Admin groups.)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349200 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Domain Admin groups.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenybatchLogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349201 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Enterprise Admin groups.
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenybatchLogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253493 - The "Deny log on as a service" user right on Windows 11 domain-joined workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253493r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberWorkstation
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000080
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a service" right defines accounts that are denied log on as a service.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a DoS.
          Check Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny log on as a service" right , this is a finding:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins Group
          Domain Admins Group
          Fix Text:This requirement is applicable to domain-joined systems, for standalone systems this is NA.

          Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on as a service" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins Group
          Domain Admins Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471

          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on as a service" right defines accounts that are denied log on as a service.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a DoS.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Deny log on as a service - Domain Admins)
              • false (Deny log on as a service - Enterprise Admins)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349300 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349300 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on as a service
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyservicelogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349301 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false

          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)

          V-253494 - The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253494r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000085
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on locally" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny log on locally" right, this is a finding.

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins Group
          Domain Admins Group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          All Systems:
          Guests Group
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on locally" to include the following:

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins Group
          Domain Admins Group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          All Systems:
          Guests Group
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253494
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000085 - The "Deny log on locally" user right on workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on locally" right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                  • false (Deny log on locally - Domain Admins)
                  • false (Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins)
                • false (System is not a member of a domain)
              • false (Deny log on locally - Guests)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349402 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on locally - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on locally
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349403 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Deny log on locally
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'
          Collected Item/State Result:

          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349401 (wmi57_test)
          Result:false
          Title:System is not a member of a domain
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
          Object Comment:

          • check = 'namespace' must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
          • check = 'wql' must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'

          State Comment:Standalone Workstation
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all domainrole must be equal to '0'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000300 (wmi57_state)
          State Comment:Standalone Server
          State Requirements:
          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
            • all domainrole must be equal to '2'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]

          domainrole = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sedenyinteractivelogonright' must be equal to '1'

          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for Guests

          V-253495 - The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows 11 workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253495r1137691_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000090
          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
          CCI-002314 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

          If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the Everyone group must be assigned this right to prevent all access.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.

          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If the following groups or accounts are not defined for the "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right, this is a finding:

          If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the "Everyone" group can replace all of the groups listed below.

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins group
          Domain Admins group
          Local account (see Note below)

          All Systems:
          Guests group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" to include the following:

          If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, assign the Everyone group this right to prevent all access.

          Domain Systems Only:
          Enterprise Admins group
          Domain Admins group
          Local account (see Note below)

          All Systems:
          Guests group

          Privileged Access Workstations (PAWs) dedicated to the management of Active Directory are exempt from denying the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups. (See the Windows Privileged Access Workstation STIG for PAW requirements.)

          Note: "Local account" is a built-in security group used to assign user rights and permissions to all local accounts.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253495
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000090 - The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right on Windows 11 workstations must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts on domain systems and unauthenticated access on all systems.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

          If Remote Desktop Services is not used by the organization, the Everyone group must be assigned this right to prevent all access.

          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                  • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                    • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins)
                    • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Enterprise Admins)
                    • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Local account)
                    • true (System is a Windows non-DC domain-joined system)
                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation or Server)
                • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Guests)
              • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Everyone)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349501 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'security_principle' must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)

          V-253505 - The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253505r958726_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-UR-000160
          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to over-write more current data.
          Check Text:Verify the effective setting in Local Group Policy Editor.
          Run "gpedit.msc".

          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment.

          If any groups or accounts other than the following are granted the "Restore files and directories" user right, this is a finding:

          Administrators
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> "Restore files and directories" to only include the following groups or accounts:

          Administrators
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253505
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-UR-000160 - The "Restore files and directories" user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

          Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to over-write more current data.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false (Restore files and directories - Administrators)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350500 (accesstoken_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Restore files and directories - Administrators
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
          Object Comment:Administrators
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          Exclude Items If:
          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350500 (accesstoken_state)
          State Comment:Restore files and directories
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'serestoreprivilege' must be equal to '0'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • serestoreprivilege equals '1' for: MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService, Backup Operators
          • serestoreprivilege equals '0' for RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, Everyone, Administrator, Hyper-V Administrators, Access Control Assistance Operators, Power Users, Guests, Event Log Readers, IUSR, SYSTEM, User Mode Hardware Operators, DIALUP, DefaultAccount, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, NETWORK, NETWORK SERVICE, MHL-PW06M2V2\Offer Remote Assistance Helpers, INTERACTIVE, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, RESTRICTED SERVICES\PrintSpoolerService, Distributed COM Users, Cryptographic Operators, CREATOR GROUP, SERVICE, ANONYMOUS LOGON, OpenSSH Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, WDAGUtilityAccount, Guest, Network Configuration Operators, Remote Desktop Users, Replicator, Remote Management Users, IIS_IUSRS, Performance Monitor Users, System Managed Accounts Group, TERMINAL SERVER USER, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER, MHL-PW06M2V2\ConfigMgr Remote Control Users, LOCAL SERVICE, Users, PROXY, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Authenticated Users, Performance Log Users, Device Owners
          Result Analysis:serestoreprivilege : MHL-PW06M2V2\DCProtectService = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
          serestoreprivilege : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

          V-257770 - Windows 11 must have command line process auditing events enabled for failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-257770r958412_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000585
          Identities:CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
          Description: When this policy setting is enabled, the operating system generates audit events when a process fails to start and the name of the program or user that created it.

          These audit events can assist in understanding how a computer is being used and tracking user activity.
          Check Text:Ensure Audit Process Creation auditing has been enabled:

          Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policy >> Detailed Tracking >> Audit Process Creation.

          If "Audit Process Creation" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Go to Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >>Security Settings>> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Detailed Tracking >> Set "Audit Process Creation" to "Failure".
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:257770
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000585 - Windows 11 must have command line process auditing events enabled.
          Description:When this policy setting is enabled, the operating system generates audit events when a process is created (starts) and the name of the program or user that created it.

          These audit events can help with understanding how a computer is being used and tracking user activity.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false (Audit - Process Creation - Failure only)
              • false (Audit - Process Creation - Success and Failure)
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25777000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:Audit - Process Creation - Failure only
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25777000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:Audit - Process Creation - Failure only
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'process_creation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-278926 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit file system failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278926r1135296_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000581
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Check Text:Verify that Audit File System auditing has been enabled:

          Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit File System.

          If "Audit File System" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File System" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278926
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000581 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit file system failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit File System' is set to 'Failure')
              • false ('Audit File System' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit File System' is set to 'Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:27894200 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'File System' is set to 'Failure'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'file_system' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'
          Collected Item/State Result:
          [ false ]
          • credential_validation equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • kerberos_authentication_service equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_service_ticket_operations equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • kerberos_ticket_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_logon_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • application_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • computer_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • distribution_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • other_account_management_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • security_group_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • user_account_management equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • dpapi_activity equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • process_creation equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • process_termination equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • rpc_events equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_access equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_changes equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • detailed_directory_service_replication equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • account_lockout equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_extended_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_main_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • ipsec_quick_mode equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logoff equals 'AUDIT_NONE'
          • logon equals 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'
          • network_policy_server equals 'AUDIT_NONE'

          V-278927 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit file system successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278927r1135299_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000582
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Check Text:Verify that Audit File System auditing has been enabled:

          Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit File System.

          If "Audit File System" is not set to "Success", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit File System" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278927
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000582 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit file system successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit File System' is set to 'Success')
              • false ('Audit File System' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894300 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit File System' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          V-278928 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit handle manipulation failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278928r1135302_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000583
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Check Text:Verify that Audit Handle Manipulation auditing has been enabled:

          Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Handle Manipulation.

          If "Audit Handle Manipulation" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Handle Manipulation" with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278928
          Definition Result:false

          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.

          V-278929 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit handle manipulation successes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278929r1135305_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000584
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Check Text:Verify that Audit Handle Manipulation auditing has been enabled:

          Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Handle Manipulation.

          If "Audit Handle Manipulation" is not set to "Success", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> "Audit Handle Manipulation" with "Success" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278929
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000584 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit handle manipulation successes.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success')
              • false ('Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:27894500 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
          State Comment:'Handle Manipulation' is set to 'Success'
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'handle_manipulation' must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'

          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.

          Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Object Access >> Audit Registry.

          V-278933 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit sensitive privilege use failures.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-278933r1141919_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-AU-000588
          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Check Text:Verify that Audit Sensitive Privilege Use auditing has been enabled:

          Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> Audit Sensitive Privilege Use.

          If "Audit Sensitive Privilege Use" is not set to "Failure", this is a finding.
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Privilege Use >> Audit Sensitive Privilege Use with "Failure" selected.
          Severity:medium
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:278933
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-AU-000588 - Windows 11 must be configured to audit sensitive privileged use failures.
          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. Credential validation records events related to validation tests on credentials for a user account logon.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
              • false ('Audit Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Failure')
              • false ('Audit Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:27894900 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Audit Sensitive Privilege Use' is set to 'Failure'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • Collect any available items.

          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'sensitive_privilege_use' must be equal to 'AUDIT_FAILURE'

        Detailed Results: Low Severity (CAT III)

          V-253355 - The system must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) generated routes.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253355r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000030
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via shortest path first.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\

          Value Name: EnableICMPRedirect

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes" to "Disabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253355
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000030 - The system must be configured to prevent Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirects from overriding Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) generated routes.
          Description:Allowing ICMP redirect of routes can lead to traffic not being routed properly. When disabled, this forces ICMP to be routed via shortest path first.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335500 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'MSS: (EnableICMPRedirect) Allow ICMP redirects to override OSPF generated routes' is set to 'Disabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335500 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'EnableICMPRedirect'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '0'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\EnableICMPRedirect'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253356 - The system must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253356r958902_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000035
          Identities:CCI-002385 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-5; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-5 a)
          Description: Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the servers WINS resolution capability.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters\

          Value Name: NoNameReleaseOnDemand

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 1
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MSS (Legacy) >> "MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow the computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers" to "Enabled".

          This policy setting requires the installation of the MSS-Legacy custom templates included with the STIG package. "MSS-Legacy.admx" and "MSS-Legacy.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253356
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000035 - The system must be configured to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers.
          Description:Configuring the system to ignore name release requests, except from WINS servers, prevents a denial of service (DoS) attack. The DoS consists of sending a NetBIOS name release request to the server for each entry in the server's cache, causing a response delay in the normal operation of the servers WINS resolution capability.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335600 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'MSS: (NoNameReleaseOnDemand) Allow computer to ignore NetBIOS name release requests except from WINS servers' is set to 'Enabled'
          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335600 (registry_object)
          Object Comment: 
          Object Requirements:
          • check = 'hive' must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
          • check = 'key' must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters'
          • check = 'name' must be equal to 'NoNameReleaseOnDemand'
          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
          State Comment: 
          State Requirements:
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'type' must be equal to 'reg_dword'
          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', check = all 'value' must be equal to '1'
          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netbt\Parameters\NoNameReleaseOnDemand'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

          V-253384 - The setting to allow Microsoft accounts to be optional for modern style apps must be enabled.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253384r991589_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000170
          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
          Description: Control of credentials and the system must be maintained within the enterprise. Enabling this setting allows enterprise credentials to be used with modern style apps that support this, instead of Microsoft accounts.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\

          Value Name: MSAOptional

          Value Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000001 (1)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> App Runtime >> "Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA
          Type:DPMS Target
          Subject:Microsoft Windows 11
          Identifier:5471
          Definitions:
          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows11:def:253384
          Definition Result:false
          Title:WN11-CC-000170 - The setting to allow Microsoft accounts to be optional for modern style apps must be enabled.
          Description:Control of credentials and the system must be maintained within the enterprise. Enabling this setting allows enterprise credentials to be used with modern style apps that support this, instead of Microsoft accounts.
          Class:compliance
          Tests:
          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
              • false ('Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional' is set to 'Enabled')
          Tests:
          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338400 (registry_test)
          Result:false
          Title:'Allow Microsoft accounts to be optional' is set to 'Enabled'

          V-253425 - Windows 11 must be configured to prevent users from receiving suggestions for third-party or additional applications.

          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-253425r958478_rule
          Test Type:Automated
          Rule Result:Fail
          Version:WN11-CC-000390
          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
          Description: Windows spotlight features may suggest apps and content from third-party software publishers in addition to Microsoft apps and content.
          Check Text:If the following registry value does not exist or is not configured as specified, this is a finding:

          Registry Hive: HKEY_CURRENT_USER
          Registry Path: \SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent\

          Value Name: DisableThirdPartySuggestions

          Type: REG_DWORD
          Value: 0x00000001 (1)
          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates. >> Windows Components >> Cloud Content >> "Do not suggest third-party content in Windows spotlight" to "Enabled".
          Severity:low
          Weight:10.0
          Reference:
          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows 11
          Publisher:DISA

          Description:Windows spotlight features may suggest apps and content from third-party software publishers in addition to Microsoft apps and content.

          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
            • false (Check all enabled user profiles HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent, verify DisableThirdPartySuggestions is REG_DWORD = 1)
